attacking_style: Attacks 3-5km from summit on long, sustained climbs, seeking gradual distancing rather than explosive accelerations. Less effective on steep, short climbs or final kilometers requiring punch.
team_tactics: Movistar/Arkea often controls tempo with domestiques early but Quintana rarely capitalizes on their work. Sometimes sacrificed for team leaders (Valverde). Post-Movistar, hunts stages from breakaways rather than GC positioning.
terrain_preference: Long, sustained alpine climbs where steady power dominates. Surprisingly attentive in crosswind echelons. Exposed on steep gradients requiring explosive acceleration, time trials (3+ minute losses), and technical gravel/descents where hesitation costs time.
vulnerability: Third-week fade pattern (dropped multiple times late Vuelta 2019). Poor positioning leads to crashes/missed splits. Time trials catastrophic for GC defense. Cannot follow explosive counter-attacks after initiating moves. Declining pure climbing power vs. younger rivals.
key_patterns: Attacks 3-5km from summit on sustained climbs, seeking gradual separation (Galibier, Granon examples)
Crosswind attentiveness unusual for pure climber, made front echelon splits twice (2019 Vuelta, 2022 Paris-Nice)
Third-week decline visible: dropped repeatedly late 2019 Vuelta after strong first two weeks
Time trial weakness severe: 3+ minute loss 2019 Vuelta, incompatible with GC podium defense
Positioning errors persistent: caught in crashes, missed intermediate sprint bonuses despite team setup
Career-stage shift: attacking from breakaways for stage wins rather than GC contention post-2021
form_trajectory: negative: Shifted from GC contention to breakaway stage hunting by 2021-2022. Tactical nous compensating for declining climbing power. Granon attack showed flashes but inconsistent.
